hero.title: A proper component landing page for the calendar, not a poster.
hero.body: CalendarCN packages a real scheduling surface for shadcn apps: reusable exports, typed state contracts, token-driven styling, and an interactive demo that shows the component working as shipped.
points: Shadcn-ready surfaces that inherit your tokens
Month, week, day, and agenda views on one event model
Drag, resize, resource planning, and keyboard support built in

snapshot.title: Built to sit inside your existing app shell.
snapshot.body: The calendar uses the same design tokens as the rest of the repo, so the landing page can focus on the exported component surface instead of inventing a separate brand world.

components.title: The page now sells the component library directly.
components.body: These are the pieces the repo actually exports. The landing page stays anchored in the real API instead of wrapping the calendar in unrelated presentation work.

items.name: CalendarRoot
items.title: The full scheduler shell
items.body: One entry point for date state, view switching, event operations, resources, and selection.
items.detail: Use it when you want the complete scheduling experience without wiring each view by hand.

items.name: CalendarToolbar
items.title: Navigation without extra glue
items.body: Previous, next, today, and view switching are already shaped for the calendar state model.
items.detail: It stays easy to restyle because the controls follow the same token system as the rest of the project.

items.name: Views
items.title: Month, week, day, and agenda
items.body: The exported views share the same event contracts, so the surface changes without changing your data layer.
items.detail: That keeps demos, embedded schedules, and focused planning views aligned.

items.name: Types + slots
items.title: Library-friendly customization
items.body: Typed events, resources, and slot-level class hooks make it practical to adapt the calendar to an existing app shell.
items.detail: You can keep the behavior and re-skin the surface instead of forking the component.

capabilities.title: The surface behaves like a real scheduler out of the box.
capabilities.body: The value here is not just rendering events. It is shipping a calendar that already supports the interactions people expect once the schedule starts changing.

items.title: One model across every view
items.body: Switch between planning and execution views without inventing separate state shapes for each surface.

items.title: Direct manipulation
items.body: Create on empty time, drag between slots, and resize from the edge where the schedule is already visible.

items.title: Practical interaction defaults
items.body: Selection, keyboard nudging, and focused editing are already part of the component instead of follow-up work.

items.title: Token-first styling
items.body: The calendar sits on top of the same `background`, `card`, `border`, and `primary` tokens as the rest of a shadcn app.

integration.title: Fits the way a shadcn app is usually assembled.
integration.body: The calendar does not need a parallel styling system. It already matches the token and component conventions the starter is using, and the extension points are specific enough for real product work.
items: Handlers for create, move, resize, navigation, and selection.
Resource-aware planning for teams, tracks, rooms, or calendars.

demo.title: One live calendar, two synced theme modes.
demo.body: Use the navbar toggle to switch between white and dark. The whole landing page and the scheduler surface stay in sync while the component behavior remains unchanged.

footer.title: Cleaner landing page, same component underneath.
footer.body: The presentation layer now points back to the reusable calendar primitives and the live scheduler instead of competing with them.

externalCta.label: See Gradual Blur on React Bits
